title: Fresh Gooey German Salmon Dumplings
description: A singleserve German-inspired salmon dumpling thats creamy, savory, and finished with a pinch of red pepper flakes for subtle heat.
instructions: 1. Heat a large non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add the butter, letting it melt and lightly brown.

2. While the butter is heating, combine the salmon cubes, yogurt, Parmesan, almond flour, egg, lemon zest, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Mix until a cohesive dough forms; if the mixture is too sticky, add a splash of milk or more flour.
3. With wet hands, scoop tablespoon‑sized portions of the dough and roll them into smooth balls, flattening slightly to help them stay together during cooking.
4. Place the dumplings in the hot skillet, spacing them so they don’t touch; sear for 2–3 minutes on each side until golden brown and the salmon reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
5. Reduce the heat to low and cover the skillet with a lid; let the dumplings steam for an additional 2 minutes to finish cooking through.
6. Remove the dumplings from the skillet and serve hot, optionally garnished with a sprinkle of fresh dill or additional lemon zest.
7. Pair with a simple salad of mixed greens and a light vinaigrette for a complete meal.
